improvement: Composite deployment evidence now rejects HTTPS lookalike domains and requires Worker URLs under .workers.dev and Pages URLs under .pages.dev.

verification.route: isolated Node 22 execution of logic-equivalent validator cases
cases: valid Cloudflare worker and pages hostnames accepted
verification.repository_status: No Cloudflare deployment workflow status was available on the test commit. Existing Vercel checks failed because of Vercel build-rate limits and are unrelated to this Cloudflare validation slice.

observed: The prior composite validator required HTTPS URLs but did not bind them to Cloudflare deployment domains.
The committed validator now parses URLs and enforces provider-specific hostname suffixes.
inferred: Provider-bound hostname validation reduces acceptance of fabricated or misrouted deployment evidence.
proposed: Add custom-domain allowlists only when they are explicitly configured and cryptographically or operationally bound to the deployment.
unresolved: The actual Cloudflare Pages hostname and deployed commit remain unverified because external Cloudflare credentials and environment configuration are still absent.

privacy_boundary: Contains repository paths, commit identifiers, test outcomes, and infrastructure semantics only. No private chat, health, credential, or personal data is included.
limits: Hostname suffix validation is infrastructure evidence, not scientific or medical proof.
A .workers.dev or .pages.dev hostname alone does not prove control, content identity, or deployment freshness; the existing identity and commit-bound proof checks remain required.
